info.name: Oraclous AI — All Services + Gateway
info.description:
Full API surface across all Oraclous backend services, callable through the application-gateway (the real entrypoint) or directly per service.

## Quick start
1. Select the **Oraclous (local)** environment.
2. Run **Auth & Identity → POST /v1/auth/register** (or login) — it captures `{{access_token}}` automatically.
3. Run **GET /v1/auth/me** to capture `{{org_id}}` and `{{user_id}}`.
4. Every other request inherits the bearer token and targets `{{base_url}}` (defaults to the gateway, `http://localhost:8006`).

## Hitting a service directly (bypass the gateway)
Change `{{base_url}}` to a service var, e.g. `{{capreg_url}}` — but note services run in **gateway auth mode** in the full stack, so a direct call needs the `X-Principal-*`/`X-Internal-Key` headers the gateway would inject. Use the gateway path for normal use; direct is for debugging.

## Routing
- Business routes → `{{base_url}}` (gateway proxies the prefix to the right service).
- **Health checks** → each service directly.
- **Internal** (`/internal/*`) → direct + `X-Internal-Key` (never gateway-exposed).

item.name: Auth & Identity

request.description: Register a new user by email+password; returns access+refresh token bundle (201).
Authenticate with email+password (optional X-Organisation-Id header selects active org); returns token bundle.
Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access+refresh bundle.
Re-issue the session for a different org (target org from X-Organisation-Id header, validated against membership). Requires a user access bearer.
Change the authenticated user's password (204). Requires a user access bearer.
Return the authenticated USER principal (id, email, organisation_id). Requires a user access bearer.
Validate the bearer and echo {valid, sub, principal_type, organisation_id}. Requires a user access bearer.
List the OAuth providers that have client credentials configured (google/github/notion).
Return the provider authorize URL (PKCE) for the given provider; requires ?redirect_uri= query param. 503 if provider unconfigured.
Exchange the OAuth code for app tokens (returned in body, never URL); requires ?code= and ?state= query params.
Create an organisation owned by the caller (201). Requires a user access bearer.
List organisations the authenticated user belongs to.
Get a single org the user is a member of (404/403 otherwise).
Update an org's name/description/logo_url (admin-gated in the service).
List members of an org the caller belongs to.
Change a member's role (admin|member); owner-gated in the service.
Remove a member from an org (204); admin/owner-gated in the service.
Create an org invitation; returns the raw invite token exactly once (201). Admin-gated.
List invitations for an org. Admin-gated.
Revoke an org invitation (204). Admin-gated.
Peek invitation metadata by raw token (in body, not URL); rate-limited on token prefix. No auth.
Accept an invitation by raw token (in body); joins the inviting org. Rate-limited; requires a user access bearer.
Exchange a raw agent/service-account credential for a short-lived JWT (rate-limited on credential prefix; bad cred -> 401). App-root route, defined in app/factory.py; not behind a gateway prefix.

App-root /me (distinct from /v1/auth/me): resolves an AGENT/service_account bearer to its principal; rejects revoked agents (T2). Defined in app/factory.py; needs a machine-principal bearer. Note: collides in name with /v1/auth/me which serves the USER principal.

item.description: Routes for auth-service (host :8005). Business routes go through the gateway ({{base_url}}).

item.name: Credentials

request.description: Create a credential for the authenticated user/org (owner bound from principal; body.user_id ignored). Returns metadata only.
List which providers the authenticated user has connected (org+user scoped from principal).
List catalogue data sources unlocked by the authenticated user's connected providers.
Get one credential's metadata by id (org+user scoped; 404 masks cross-org/cross-user/unknown).
List the authenticated user's own credentials, optionally filtered by tool_id (body.user_id ignored).
Update a credential (org+user scoped from principal). Returns metadata only.
Delete a credential by id (org+user scoped from principal). 204 No Content.

item.description: Routes for credential-broker-service (host :8002). Business routes go through the gateway ({{base_url}}).

item.name: Capability Registry

request.description: List capability descriptors for the caller org (optional ?kind= filter).
Register a capability descriptor (OHM manifest JSONB).
Match capabilities by a list of capability names.
Get a single capability descriptor by id (org-scoped; 404 masks cross-org).
Replace a capability descriptor's manifest.
Delete a capability descriptor (204).
List tool-kind descriptors for the caller org.
Register a tool (id derived deterministically from descriptor name/version/category; idempotent).
Get a single tool descriptor by id.
List tool instances for the caller org.
Create a configured tool instance from a capability (owner = authenticated principal).
Get a single tool instance by id (org-scoped).
Map credential_type -> credential_id (credential lives in the credential-broker).
Validate an instance's execution readiness (returns a checks/errors report).
Synchronously dispatch a configured instance; resolves credentials, writes provenance, returns execution.
Fetch a past execution record (provenance lookup; org-scoped).

item.description: Routes for capability-registry-service (host :8001). Business routes go through the gateway ({{base_url}}).

item.name: Knowledge Graph

request.description: Create a new knowledge graph for the caller's org.
List the caller's graphs (org/owner scoped).
Get one graph by id; 404 if not found in the caller's scope.
Update a graph's name/description.
Delete a graph; 204 on success, 404 if not in scope.
Ingest inline text/structured content; creates a job and enqueues async processing (202).
Upload a document file (multipart form: file=@..., optional recipe_id) for async ingestion (202).
Poll an ingestion job's status/progress; 404 if graph or job not in scope.
List ingested documents (job rows) for a graph.
Get the graph's label ontology (allowed_labels + mode).
Set the graph's label ontology (allowed_labels list + open|strict|coerce mode).
Store a format-0.2 ingestion recipe (validated server-side); 422 on invalid recipe.
List stored recipe summaries (org scoped).
Get the latest version of a recipe by id; 404 if not found.

item.description: Routes for knowledge-graph-service (host :8003). Business routes go through the gateway ({{base_url}}).

item.name: Knowledge Retriever

request.description: Semantic (vector) search over the org-scoped graph for a given graph_id.
Full-text keyword search over the org-scoped graph for a given graph_id.
Hybrid (vector + full-text) search over the org-scoped graph for a given graph_id.
Bounded {nodes, edges} graph slice for visualisation; query param limit (1..1000, default 250).
1-hop neighbourhood of a node; query param top_k (1..200, default 25).
Entities whose validity covers the as_of instant; required query param as_of (ISO timestamp), top_k (1..200, default 25).

item.description: Routes for knowledge-retriever-service (host :8004). Business routes go through the gateway ({{base_url}}).

item.name: Harness Runtime (R4)

request.description: Run an inline (or referenced) OHM harness to completion through the agent tool-use loop; returns the execution row. Supply exactly one of manifest_yaml / manifest / manifest_ref.
List all harness executions for the caller's organisation (org-scoped from the principal).
Fetch a single prior harness execution by id, scoped to the caller's organisation; 404 if not found.
Human task board: list PENDING human-actor assignments for the caller's organisation.

item.description: Routes for harness-runtime-service (host :8007). Business routes go through the gateway ({{base_url}}).

item.name: Gateway

request.description: Aggregates each upstream's /health into per-service status plus an overall rollup; always returns 200, body reflects substrate state.

Catch-all reverse-proxy: this service IS the gateway. It forwards any non-/health request to its upstream by longest-prefix match (route_table.py): /v1/auth,/v1/orgs,/v1/invitations,/oauth->auth-service; /credentials->credential-broker; /api/v1/graphs,/api/v1/recipes->knowledge-graph; /v1/search,/v1/graph->knowledge-retriever; /api/v1/capabilities,/api/v1/tools,/api/v1/instances,/api/v1/executions->capability-registry; /v1/harnesses->harness-runtime. Edge auth terminates ONCE here: /v1/auth and /oauth are public (token-issuance), every other routed prefix requires a verified bearer Principal (401 fail-closed pre-forward). Methods GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/OPTIONS/HEAD. Upstream 4xx/5xx is normalised to the ORA-37 error envelope (body never relayed). No own business logic — it is the reverse proxy.

item.description: Routes for application-gateway-service (host :8006). Business routes go through the gateway ({{base_url}}).

item.name: Health checks (direct to each service)

request.description: Liveness probe; returns {"status":"ok"}. App-root route, not under any gateway prefix (used by the compose healthcheck).
Liveness/health probe; returns {status, service}. App-level route, not under a router prefix; not edge-routed (compose healthcheck hits it directly).
Dependency-free liveness probe (healthy even when Postgres is down).
Dependency-free liveness probe under the /api/v1 prefix.
Instance health view (alias of validate-execution readiness report).
Liveness probe; returns {status, service}. Unauthenticated; used by the docker healthcheck.
Liveness probe for docker healthcheck; returns status and service name.
Liveness probe; returns service name/status. Unauthenticated (docker healthcheck). Not under the /v1/harnesses gateway prefix.
Dependency-free liveness probe; returns 200 with status/service/version even when every upstream is down. Served locally, never proxied (health router included before the catch-all).

item.description: Liveness probes hit each service directly on its own port.

item.name: Internal (service-to-service)

request.description: Create an agent/service-account credential and return the raw secret exactly once (201). X-Internal-Key gated (service-to-service).
Revoke all active credentials for an agent (idempotent). X-Internal-Key gated.
Return the static provider/data-source capability catalogue (X-Internal-Key gated).
Service-to-service: resolve a (refreshed) provider OAuth token for org+user+provider with required scopes (X-Internal-Key gated).
Service-to-service: resolve a provider token scoped to a named data source (scopes from catalogue, never trusted input). X-Internal-Key gated.
Service-to-service: mint a delegated token for a member→agent with scopes+expiry; returns the raw bearer exactly once. X-Internal-Key gated.
Service-to-service: validate a delegated token per-use against org, requesting agent, and requested scopes. X-Internal-Key gated.
Service-to-service: revoke a delegated token by id (org-scoped); returns {revoked_count}. X-Internal-Key gated.
Service-to-service: return a stored credential's DECRYPTED payload by id (org-scoped) for non-OAuth secret resolution (connection_string/api_key). 404 masks cross-org. X-Internal-Key gated.
Service-to-service: org+graph-scoped label/relationship shape of a graph (still requires a principal via UserIdDep; org filtered).

item.description: Internal /internal/* routes — NOT exposed through the gateway. Call the service directly with X-Internal-Key. For debugging only.
